name: Gunma Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: dignity, tradition, and the noble character of the prefecture
purity and the bright future of Gunma

description: A deep purple field charged at the centre with a white emblem of three rounded shapes arranged around a small central disc, symbolising the three principal mountains of the prefecture.
history: Gunma's prefectural symbol was adopted on 28 October 1926. The flag, which places the white emblem on a purple field, has been in use as the prefectural flag since the same period.

features.symbolism: a deep purple chosen to represent the dignity and history of the prefecture
three white circles arranged around a central dot, stylising the three sacred mountains of Gunma (Mt. Akagi, Mt. Haruna and Mt. Myogi); the design also evokes the kanji 群 ('group') of Gunma.
